title: T-010 — Fiscal calendar engine (generate_fiscal_periods function + fiscal_periods table consumer) (12-REPORTING §6 D-RPT-2)
prompt:
# T-010 — Fiscal calendar engine

PRD: docs/prd/12-REPORTING-PRD.md §6 D-RPT-2 (4-4-5 / 4-5-4 / 5-4-4 / calendar_months), §5 Biznesowe (fiscal calendar config required pre-E4), §11 V-RPT-QUERY-4 (week_ending Saturday)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Implement `app.generate_fiscal_periods(org_id, year)` Postgres function that materialises P1-P13 rows into `fiscal_periods` reference table (owned by 02-SETTINGS §8 per D-RPT-2). Reads `organization_settings.fiscal_calendar_type`; defaults to calendar_months if unset. Service helper to look up the current period for a given (org, date). Used by P2 Period Reports (RPT-P2-006) and by V-RPT-QUERY-5 validation; scaffolded in P1 so consumer dashboards can read fiscal context.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0087_reporting_fiscal_calendar.sql` — creates `app.generate_fiscal_periods(org_id uuid, year int) RETURNS void` SECURITY DEFINER function that:
   - Reads `organization_settings.fiscal_calendar_type` (FK to 02-SETTINGS).
   - Computes period boundaries per type (4-4-5, 4-5-4, 5-4-4, or calendar_months).
   - INSERTs P1..P12 (or P13 for 4-4-5 fiscal year) rows into `fiscal_periods` (org_id, year, period_no, start_date, end_date).
   - Idempotent: `ON CONFLICT (org_id, year, period_no) DO NOTHING`.
2. If `fiscal_periods` table is not yet owned by 02-SETTINGS, verify schema via `information_schema` before creating; if missing, scope is to create it here with org_id + RLS (cite 02-SETTINGS §8 dependency in coverage.md note).
3. Service helper `packages/reporting/src/queries/fiscal-period.ts` — `getCurrentFiscalPeriod(orgId, date)` returns `{year, period_no, start_date, end_date}`; throws `FiscalCalendarNotConfiguredError` if no rows exist (per V-RPT-QUERY-5).
4. Vitest contract test: (a) 4-4-5 generates 13 periods totalling 364/365 days correctly, (b) calendar_months fallback generates 12 periods (Jan..Dec), (c) ON CONFLICT idempotency, (d) RLS isolation on fiscal_periods, (e) FiscalCalendarNotConfiguredError thrown when no rows.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0087_reporting_fiscal_calendar.sql [create]
- packages/reporting/src/queries/fiscal-period.ts [create]
- packages/reporting/__tests__/fiscal-calendar.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration 0087 runs and a 2026 4-4-5 config exists for org_A, when `app.generate_fiscal_periods(org_A_id, 2026)` runs, then 13 rows are inserted into fiscal_periods with consecutive start/end dates totalling 364 or 365 days; period_no ∈ [1..13]; week_ending is Saturday per V-RPT-QUERY-4.
2. Given an org with no fiscal_calendar_type set, when generate_fiscal_periods runs, then calendar_months fallback inserts 12 rows (Jan..Dec) per D-RPT-2.
3. Given generate_fiscal_periods runs twice for the same (org, year), when the second call completes, then the row count is unchanged (ON CONFLICT idempotency).
4. Given `getCurrentFiscalPeriod(org_A, '2026-03-15')` is called, when periods exist, then it returns the period whose range contains that date; when no periods, throws FiscalCalendarNotConfiguredError.
5. Given fiscal_periods is queried under cross-org session, when rows are returned, then RLS isolation passes (zero leaks).

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: `packages/reporting/__tests__/fiscal-calendar.test.ts` covers 5 ACs with testcontainers Postgres + seeded organization_settings rows.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/reporting test fiscal-calendar`.
- GREEN: write migration + Postgres function + service helper.

## Risk red lines
- Do not skip the schema verification step — `fiscal_periods` ownership is declared in 02-SETTINGS §8 reference tables; verify presence before writing migration body (per kira_hq_red_prompt_verify_schema memory).
- Do not hardcode period boundaries — derive from fiscal_calendar_type config per org per D-RPT-2.
- Do not generate P13 for non-4-4-5 calendars — 4-5-4 / 5-4-4 / calendar_months produce 12 periods only.
- Do not let function fail silently on missing config — use calendar_months fallback (D-RPT-2 "jesli org nie skonfigurowal → domyslnie calendar_months").
- Do not let week_ending dates fall on non-Saturday per V-RPT-QUERY-4 (ISO 8601 convention).
- Do not run generate_fiscal_periods in a hot request path — long-running; call from worker (T-005-style cron) on org-creation event or admin trigger.

pipeline_inputs.description: generate_fiscal_periods Postgres function + fiscal_periods consumer helper supporting 4-4-5 / 4-5-4 / 5-4-4 / calendar_months. P1 scaffold for P2 Period Reports.
pipeline_inputs.details: Function reads organization_settings.fiscal_calendar_type. ON CONFLICT idempotent. Service helper enforces RLS + throws on missing config. Verify fiscal_periods ownership in 02-SETTINGS §8 before creating table.

Do not implement Multi-granularity time selector (P2 D-RPT-4)

Given edge and compliance cases are exercised, when each condition is triggered, then it returns the period whose range contains the date; when no periods, throws FiscalCalendarNotConfiguredError and row count is unchanged.
